get.description: For the specified scope, get the current quota requests for a one year period ending at the time is made. Use the **oData** filter to select quota requests.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.

parameters.name: $filter
$top
parameters.description: Number of records to return.

parameters.name: $skiptoken
parameters.description: The **Skiptoken** parameter is used only if a previous operation returned a partial result. If a previous response contains a **nextLink** element, its value includes a **skiptoken** parameter that specifies a starting point to use for subsequent calls.

parameters.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.

ETag.description: Current entity state version. Should be treated as opaque and used to make conditional HTTP requests.

get.description: Get the quota limit of a resource. The response can be used to determine the remaining quota to calculate a new quota limit that can be submitted with a PUT request.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.

parameters.name: resourceName
parameters.description:
Resource name for a given resource provider. For example:
- SKU name for Microsoft.Compute
- SKU or TotalLowPriorityCores for Microsoft.MachineLearningServices
 For Microsoft.Network PublicIPAddresses.

ETag.description: Current entity state version. Should be treated as opaque and used to make conditional HTTP requests.

put.description:
Create or update the quota limit for the specified resource with the requested value. To update the quota, follow these steps:
1. Use the GET operation for quotas and usages to determine how much quota remains for the specific resource and to calculate the new quota limit. These steps are detailed in [this example](https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/azure-governance-and-management/using-the-new-quota-rest-api/ba-p/2183670).
2. Use this PUT operation to update the quota limit. Please check the URI in location header for the detailed status of the request.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.

parameters.name: resourceName
parameters.description:
Resource name for a given resource provider. For example:
- SKU name for Microsoft.Compute
- SKU or TotalLowPriorityCores for Microsoft.MachineLearningServices
 For Microsoft.Network PublicIPAddresses.

parameters.name: createQuotaRequest
parameters.description: Quota request payload.

Location.description: The Location header contains the URL where the status of the long running operation can be checked.

Retry-After.description: The Retry-After header can indicate how long the client should wait before polling the operation status.

patch.description:
Update the quota limit for a specific resource to the specified value:
1. Use the Usages-GET and Quota-GET operations to determine the remaining quota for the specific resource and to calculate the new quota limit. These steps are detailed in [this example](https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/azure-governance-and-management/using-the-new-quota-rest-api/ba-p/2183670).
2. Use this PUT operation to update the quota limit. Please check the URI in location header for the detailed status of the request.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The fully qualified Azure Resource manager identifier of the resource.

parameters.name: resourceName
parameters.description:
Resource name for a given resource provider. For example:
- SKU name for Microsoft.Compute
- SKU or TotalLowPriorityCores for Microsoft.MachineLearningServices
 For Microsoft.Network PublicIPAddresses.

parameters.name: createQuotaRequest
parameters.description: Quota requests payload.

get.description: Gets the GroupQuotas for the name passed. It will return the GroupQuotas properties only. The details on group quota can be access from the group quota APIs.

parameters.description: The management group ID.

parameters.name: groupQuotaName
parameters.description: The GroupQuota name. The name should be unique for the provided context tenantId/MgId.

put.description: Creates a new GroupQuota for the name passed. A RequestId will be returned by the Service. The status can be polled periodically. The status Async polling is using standards defined at - https://github.com/Azure/azure-resource-manager-rpc/blob/master/v1.0/async-api-reference.md#asynchronous-operations. Use the OperationsStatus URI provided in Azure-AsyncOperation header, the duration will be specified in retry-after header. Once the operation gets to terminal state - Succeeded | Failed, then the URI will change to Get URI and full details can be checked.

parameters.name: managementGroupId
parameters.description: The management group ID.

parameters.name: groupQuotaName
parameters.description: The GroupQuota name. The name should be unique for the provided context tenantId/MgId.

parameters.name: GroupQuotaPutRequestBody
parameters.description: The GroupQuota body details for creation or update of a GroupQuota entity.

Azure-AsyncOperation.description: A link to the status monitor

Location.description: The Location header contains the URL where the status of the long running operation can be checked.

Retry-After.description: The Retry-After header can indicate how long the client should wait before polling the operation status.

patch.description:
Updates the GroupQuotas for the name passed. A GroupQuotas RequestId will be returned by the Service. The status can be polled periodically. The status Async polling is using standards defined at - https://github.com/Azure/azure-resource-manager-rpc/blob/master/v1.0/async-api-reference.md#asynchronous-operations. Use the OperationsStatus URI provided in Azure-AsyncOperation header, the duration will be specified in retry-after header. Once the operation gets to terminal state - Succeeded | Failed, then the URI will change to Get URI and full details can be checked.
Any change in the filters will be applicable to the future quota assignments, existing quota allocated to subscriptions from the GroupQuotas remains unchanged.

Create the GroupQuota requests for a specific ResourceProvider/Location/Resource. The resourceName properties are specified in the request body. Only 1 resource quota can be requested. Please note that patch request creates a new groupQuota request.
Use the polling API - OperationsStatus URI specified in Azure-AsyncOperation header field, with retry-after duration in seconds to check the intermediate status. This API provides the finals status with the request details and status.

get.description: Gets the GroupQuotas enforcement settings for the ResourceProvider/location. The locations, where GroupQuota enforcement is not enabled will return Not Found.

parameters.name: managementGroupId
parameters.description: The management group ID.

parameters.name: groupQuotaName
parameters.description: The GroupQuota name. The name should be unique for the provided context tenantId/MgId.

parameters.name: resourceProviderName
parameters.description: The resource provider name, such as - Microsoft.Compute. Currently only Microsoft.Compute resource provider supports this API.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: The name of the Azure region.

put.description:
Enables the GroupQuotas enforcement for the resource provider and the location specified. The resource provider will start using the group quotas as the overall quota for the subscriptions included in the GroupQuota. The subscriptions cannot request quota at subscription level since it is now part of an enforced group.
The subscriptions share the GroupQuotaLimits assigned to the GroupQuota. If the GroupQuotaLimits is used, then submit a groupQuotaLimit request for the specific resource - provider/location/resource.
Once the GroupQuota Enforcement is enabled then, it cannot be deleted or reverted back. To disable GroupQuota Enforcement -
1. Remove all the subscriptions from the groupQuota using the delete API for Subscriptions (Check the example - GroupQuotaSubscriptions_Delete).
2. Then delete the GroupQuota (Check the example - GroupQuotas_Delete).

parameters.name: managementGroupId
parameters.description: The management group ID.

parameters.name: groupQuotaName
parameters.description: The GroupQuota name. The name should be unique for the provided context tenantId/MgId.

parameters.name: resourceProviderName
parameters.description: The resource provider name, such as - Microsoft.Compute. Currently only Microsoft.Compute resource provider supports this API.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: The name of the Azure region.

parameters.name: LocationSettings
parameters.description: The GroupQuota body details for creation or update of a GroupQuota entity.

Location.description: The Location header contains the URL where the status of the long running operation can be checked.

Retry-After.description: The Retry-After header can indicate how long the client should wait before polling the operation status.

patch.description:
Enables the GroupQuotas enforcement for the resource provider and the location specified. The resource provider will start using the group quotas as the overall quota for the subscriptions included in the GroupQuota.  The subscriptions cannot request quota at subscription level since it is now part of an enforced group.
The subscriptions share the GroupQuotaLimits assigned to the GroupQuota. If the GroupQuotaLimits is used, then submit a groupQuotaLimit request for the specific resource - provider/location/resource.
Once the GroupQuota Enforcement is enabled then, it cannot be deleted or reverted back. To disable GroupQuota Enforcement -
1. Remove all the subscriptions from the groupQuota using the delete API for Subscriptions (Check the example - GroupQuotaSubscriptions_Delete).
2. Ten delete the GroupQuota (Check the example - GroupQuotas_Delete).

patch.description: Request to assign quota from group quota to a specific Subscription. The assign GroupQuota to subscriptions or reduce the quota allocated to subscription to give back the unused quota ( quota >= usages) to the groupQuota. So, this API can be used to assign Quota to subscriptions and assign back unused quota to group quota, which can be assigned to another subscriptions in the GroupQuota. User can collect unused quotas from multiple subscriptions within the groupQuota and assign the groupQuota to the subscription, where it's needed.

comment.description: Optional free-text comment supplied by the approver.

actor.description: Principal that performed the approval (typically a UPN or service principal id).

occurredAt.description: Timestamp at which the approval was recorded.

reason.description: Optional free-text reason supplied by the donor when cancelling.

limit.description: The current Group Quota Limit at the parentId level.

comment.description: Any comment related to quota request.

unit.description: The usages units, such as Count and Bytes. When requesting quota, use the **unit** value returned in the GET response in the request body of your PUT operation.

name.description: Name of the resource provided by the resource provider. This property is already included in the request URI, so it is a readonly property returned in the response.

availableLimit.description: The available Group Quota Limit at the MG level. This Group quota can be allocated to subscription(s).

GroupQuotaDetailsName.description: Name of the resource provided by the resource provider. This property is already included in the request URI, so it is a readonly property returned in the response.

IncomingQuotaTransfer.description:
Recipient-side projection of a quota transfer. The URI key `{transferId}` is the
server-generated GUID returned to the donor in the PUT response under
`properties.transferId`. The resource is read-only from the recipient side; state
changes occur through the approve and reject actions.

properties.description: Properties of the incoming quota transfer.

etag.description: "If etag is provided in the response body, it may also be provided as a header per the normal etag convention.  Entity tags are used for comparing two or more entities from the same requested resource. HTTP/1.1 uses entity tags in the etag (section 14.19), If-Match (section 14.24), If-None-Match (section 14.26), and If-Range (section 14.27) header fields.")

IncomingQuotaTransferApproveRequest.description: Request body for the recipient approve action.

comment.description: Optional free-text comment recorded on the transfer.

IncomingQuotaTransferListResult.description: The response of a IncomingQuotaTransfer list operation.

value.description: The IncomingQuotaTransfer items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

transferId.description: Server-generated identifier of the transfer (matches the URI key).

transferRef.description: Fully qualified ARM resource id of the donor-side quotaTransfers resource.

sourceSubscriptionId.description: Donor subscription id. The recipient subscription is the one in the request URI.

sourceTenantId.description: Donor tenant id, resolved by the service from the donor subscription.

billingAccountId.description: Billing account id both donor and recipient subscriptions roll up to.

amount.description: Amount being transferred in the resource's native unit.

sourceEtag.description:
ETag of the donor-side source document at the time the inbox entry was projected. Used
as the If-Match value on approve and reject requests.

approval.description:
Approval record. Populated when `transferStatus` is `Accepted` or `Completed`.
Mutually exclusive with `rejection`.

rejection.description:
Rejection record. Populated when `transferStatus` is `Rejected`.
Mutually exclusive with `approval`.

reason.description: Optional free-text reason recorded on the transfer.

QuotaTransfer.description:
A quota transfer authored on the donor side. The donor selects the URI segment
`{transferName}`; the recipient addresses the same logical transfer via the
server-generated `properties.transferId` GUID on the `incomingQuotaTransfers` URI.

etag.description: "If etag is provided in the response body, it may also be provided as a header per the normal etag convention.  Entity tags are used for comparing two or more entities from the same requested resource. HTTP/1.1 uses entity tags in the etag (section 14.19), If-Match (section 14.24), If-None-Match (section 14.26), and If-Range (section 14.27) header fields.")

QuotaTransferCancelRequest.description: Request body for the donor cancel action.

reason.description: Optional free-text reason recorded on the transfer.

QuotaTransferListResult.description: The response of a QuotaTransfer list operation.

value.description: The QuotaTransfer items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

QuotaTransferProperties.description: Donor-side properties of a quota transfer.

provisioningState.description: The status of the underlying ARM resource operation.

transferStatus.description: The business status of the transfer.

transferId.description:
Server-generated identifier the recipient uses to address the transfer on the
incomingQuotaTransfers URI.

displayName.description: Human-friendly label surfaced on customer GET responses and recipient inbox listings.

comment.description: Donor-supplied free-text rationale captured at submit time.

destinationSubscriptionId.description: Recipient subscription id. Must differ from the donor subscription.

destinationTenantId.description: Recipient tenant id, resolved by the service from the recipient subscription.

billingAccountId.description: Billing account id both donor and recipient subscriptions must roll up to.

resourceName.description:
The quota dimension being moved, scoped by the URI's target provider
(for example, `standardDv5Family` under Microsoft.Compute).

amount.description: Amount to transfer in the resource's native unit (e.g. vCPU count).

autoApprove.description:
Same-tenant one-shot opt-in. When true, the donor PUT admission-checks recipient-side
RBAC and cap at submit time and drives the transfer to terminal Completed within the
same LRO, with no recipient approve required. The outcome is reflected by
`transferStatus`: `Completed` means the auto path committed; `Pending` means it did
not (e.g. cross-tenant, missing RBAC, cap exceeded) and the recipient must approve.

expiresAt.description: Time at which a Pending transfer expires if the recipient has not approved or rejected it.

approval.description:
Approval record. Populated when `transferStatus` is `Accepted` or `Completed`.
Mutually exclusive with `cancellation`.

cancellation.description:
Cancellation record. Populated when `transferStatus` is `Cancelled`.
Mutually exclusive with `approval`.

RejectionRecord.description: Record of a rejection action on a transfer.

reason.description: Optional free-text reason supplied by the recipient when rejecting.

occurredAt.description: Timestamp at which the rejection was recorded.

values.name: Created
values.description: The quota request has been created.

values.name: Invalid
values.description: The quota request is invalid.

values.name: Succeeded
values.description: The quota request has succeeded.

values.name: Escalated
values.description: The quota request has been escalated for further review. Please file a support ticket. A support engineer will follow up.

values.name: Failed
values.description: The quota request has failed.

values.name: InProgress
values.description: The quota request is currently being processed.

values.name: Canceled
values.description: The quota request has been canceled.

TransferProvisioningState.description:
Provisioning state of the ARM long-running operation that last wrote to a quota
transfer resource (the donor PUT, or the recipient approve / reject actions).
Reflects the infrastructure outcome of that call only; the business outcome of the
transfer itself is reported separately on `transferStatus`.

values.name: Succeeded
values.description: The LRO completed successfully.

values.name: Failed
values.description: The LRO terminated with a failure.

values.name: Canceled
values.description: The LRO was canceled before it reached a terminal state.

TransferStatus.description:
Business status of a quota transfer. Distinct from `provisioningState`, which only
reports the ARM LRO outcome of the most recent call.

values.name: Pending
values.description: The transfer has been created on the donor side and is awaiting recipient action.

values.name: Accepted
values.description: The recipient has approved the transfer; quota commit is in progress.

values.name: Completed
values.description: The transfer has been applied and quota is committed at the recipient.

values.name: Cancelled
values.description: The donor cancelled the transfer before it was approved.

values.name: Rejected
values.description: The recipient rejected the transfer.

values.name: Expired
values.description: The transfer aged out before the recipient approved or rejected it.

values.name: Failed
values.description: The transfer terminated with a failure.
